sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.39 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Conjecture: Let$G$be a hyperbolic group. Then every 2-dimensional rational homology class is virtually represented by a sum of closed surface subgroups, that is, for any$H_2(G; )$there are finitely many closed oriented surfaces$S_i$and injective homomorphisms$_i: _1(S_i) G$such that$_i [S_i] = n$, where$[S_i]$denotes the image of the fundamental class of$S_i$in$H_2(G)$.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
